mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and compress corporate profit margins, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistically significant negative correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which triggers immediate equity sell-offs over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE increases exceed 0.2pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic demand, boosting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in GDPC1 and RSAFS fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in over 60% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp increases in industrial production indicate accelerating factory output and supply-side momentum, generating short-term positive equity returns during early-cycle expansion phases.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO shocks yield negative or flat 5-day forward returns during early-cycle expansions over a 2-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above 3.0% forces monetary authorities to accelerate rate hikes, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic capacity and rising household income, which supports corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no positive predictive power for 60-day forward returns, with Rank IC consistently below 0.02 across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and retail sales demonstrate synchronized strength in durable goods demand and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising HOUST and RSAFS fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production combined with rising unemployment signals a supply-demand mismatch and economic contraction, triggering rapid equity sell-offs over the next week during late-cycle downturns.
failure_condition: Negative forward 5-day returns do not materialize in more than 60% of cases where INDPRO falls and UNRATE rises simultaneously during late-cycle phases.
